title: data-us pack=memo-fetch output
description: Pack output for `data-us/scripts/pack.py --ticker {T} --pack memo-fetch`. Heavy bundle: yfinance info + 2y price + SEC EDGAR filings index (10-K/10-Q/8-K, configurable; default 8 — use --filings-limit to override) + SEC XBRL us-gaap concept facts. Single ticker only by design (SEC EDGAR is rate-limited 10 req/s).

company_info.description: yfinance info dict — same shape as snapshot.company_info (see schema-snapshot.json). Tier 2.

price_history.description: yfinance OHLCV — same shape as snapshot.price_history (see schema-snapshot.json). Tier 2.

sec_filings.description: SEC EDGAR filings index for the requested forms (Tier A — official primary source).

cik.description: 10-digit SEC CIK (may render as int or zero-padded string).

title.description: Issuer name as registered with SEC.

sec_narrative.description: Memo-feed narrative bundle (Tier A): management's filed text for the latest 10-K, latest 10-Q, and one earnings 8-K (item 2.02) per quarter for the last N quarters, per `sec_edgar_client.select_narrative_filings`'s policy. A depth-1 wrapper around per-filing `--action narrative` results (`sec_edgar_client.fetch_narrative_sections`) — see the two consumer-contract notes below before reading `failed_items`/`succeeded`/`failed`.

filings.description: One entry per SELECTED filing (accession chosen by the selection policy), in selection order (10-K, 10-Q, then 8-Ks newest-quarter-first). Each entry is `{role, quarter?: <YYYYQn> (8-K only), ...fetch_narrative_sections result}` — i.e. `accession`/`cik`/`form`/`filingDate`/`sections`/`section_count`/`narrative_status`/`failed_items`/`_cache`, OR (on acquisition failure) a loud `{role, quarter?, error, ...}` slot in place of the narrative fields.

sections.description: Per-item section objects (`item`, `text_path`, `disclosure_status`, provenance) OR per-item error slots (`item`, `error`, `error_class`) — see the consumer-contract note: a classifier that only walks dict-valued sub-fields cannot see failures nested in THIS list; use `narrative_status`/`failed_items` on this same filing entry, or the wrapper's own depth-1 `failed_items` below, instead.

error.description: Present only when the filing acquisition itself failed (no sections were ever fetched).

failed_items.description: CONSUMER CONTRACT: this is the load-bearing failure signal for the whole `sec_narrative` bundle, hoisted to THIS wrapper's own depth 1 — do NOT rely on a classifier walking into any filing's `sections` list to detect failure (`pack.py`'s own classifier walks exactly one level and cannot see into a list-valued sub-field, so a per-section failure living inside `filings[].sections` is structurally invisible to it). Combines per-section failures (from filings whose `narrative_status` was `partial`) and selection gaps (a requested role/quarter with no qualifying filing at all, e.g. no earnings 8-K found for a quarter).

accession.description: Present for a section-level failure inside an acquired filing; absent for a selection gap (no such filing existed).

item.description: The failing item id (e.g. 'Item 1A'); present for section-level failures, absent for selection gaps.

requested.description: Policy-fixed count = 2 + n_quarters (10-K + 10-Q + one 8-K per quarter), decided by the SELECTION policy — never by what came back. Reconciles by construction: `succeeded + failed == requested`.

succeeded.description: CONSUMER CONTRACT: counts FILINGS OBTAINED, not sections fully extracted. A filing that was fetched but whose own `narrative_status` is `partial` (some of its sections failed) still counts as succeeded here — the filing itself was not a gap. Its section-level failures are carried separately in `failed_items` and force this wrapper's own `_status` to `partial`. So `succeeded + failed == requested` can reconcile cleanly while some section text is still missing; do not read a clean count triple as "nothing is missing" — check `_status` and `failed_items` too.

failed.description: Filings never obtained at all: selection gaps (no qualifying filing existed) plus filings whose acquisition itself failed. `succeeded + failed == requested` by construction.

_status.description: Derived from the count triple AND each filing's own `narrative_status`: `failed` (== requested, and requested > 0) when nothing was obtained; `partial` when any filing failed outright OR any obtained filing itself reports `narrative_status: partial`; else `ok`.

sec_facts.description: SEC XBRL company-concept facts index (Tier A). When `concept` is null, returns the full index of available concepts (us-gaap + dei taxonomies) and their counts. Specific concept values can be fetched via separate sec_edgar_client.py calls — not bundled here for size.

concept.description: Specific us-gaap concept (e.g. 'Revenues') if requested; null when fetching the full concept index.

concept_counts.description: Per-taxonomy counts: typically `{us-gaap: N, dei: M}`.

us_gaap_concepts.description: List of available us-gaap concept names for this issuer (e.g. 'Revenues', 'NetIncomeLoss', 'AccountsPayable', ...).

dei_concepts.description: List of available DEI (Document and Entity Information) concept names.

history.description: T1 canonical OHLCV alias — same list reference as price_history.data. Top-level for analysis-technical. See docs/normalization-contract.md.

income_statement.description: T3 canonical income statement (annual, most-recent-first, depth 5). Per ADR-0003. Contains revenue / operating_income / ebit / net_income / gross_profit arrays plus _meta provenance.

cash_flow.description: T3 canonical cash flow statement. Contains operating_cash_flow / capex / fcf (derived = OCF - capex) / depreciation_amortization / stock_based_compensation plus _meta. Per ADR-0003.

balance_sheet.description: T3 canonical balance sheet. Contains long_term_debt / short_term_debt / total_debt (derived = LTD + STD) / cash / total_stockholders_equity / intangible_assets / goodwill plus _meta. Per ADR-0003.

shares_outstanding.description: Absolute share count from yfinance company_info.sharesOutstanding (NOT XBRL dei). T3 canonical.

current_price.description: Latest market price from yfinance company_info.regularMarketPrice. T3 canonical.

us_specific.description: US-specific extension block per Principle 3. v1 placeholders for known T3 gaps (non-GAAP EPS in 8-K narratives, segment revenue).
